scenario_title: add_rr and delete_rrset for same name/type in one UPDATE

group_1.behavior: Processes the UPDATE section as an ordered sequence, so the add_rr is applied and then removed by delete_rrset; the server replies NOERROR and the queried RRset is absent afterward.

group_2.behavior: Treats the conflicting same-message add/delete combination differently, either rejecting the UPDATE or preserving the RRset; the post-state is not absent.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3 and §3.4 define add_rr and delete_rrset individually, but do not specify intra-message ordering or normalization when both target the same owner/type in one UPDATE. That leaves both sequential semantics and set-based/conflict semantics plausible, producing different replies and/or final RRset state.
expected_divergence.test_rationale: This test makes the ambiguity observable with a minimal zone and a single UPDATE. The message first creates host2.campus.edu. A and then deletes the entire same RRset in the same UPDATE. If a server evaluates operations sequentially, the final RRset should be absent with NOERROR. If it instead rejects the conflict or handles updates as non-ordered net changes, the reply and/or final state will differ. The follow-up QUERY on host2.campus.edu. A provides a binary post-state oracle.
